name: Evergreen Hills Golf Club & Resort
notes: Address: 152 Moo 5, Tha Muang District, Kanchanaburi. Designer At-anan Yomchinda. Year 1992. 6,879 yards. Signature holes 16 and 17 feature island greens. 77-room resort popular with Korean golfers. Night golf available. ~2 hours from Bangkok, ~50 min from Kanchanaburi town.

prose.overview: Evergreen Hills Golf Club & Resort is an 18-hole, par-72 course located at 152 Moo 5, Tha Muang District, Kanchanaburi province — positioned between Kanchanaburi town and the Gulf coast route toward Hua Hin. Designed by At-anan Yomchinda and opened in 1992, the layout stretches 6,879 yards and has built a solid reputation over more than three decades of operation. Green fees are 800 THB on weekdays and 1,200 THB on weekends. Caddie fees are 300 THB and cart hire is 700 THB. Club rentals are available at 900 THB per set. The attached resort offers 77 rooms, making it a natural base for golfers who want to stay overnight rather than drive back to Bangkok on the same day. Evergreen Hills is particularly popular with Korean golfers, who make up a significant share of the resort's visitor profile.
prose.layout_and_experience: The course plays to 6,879 yards from the back tees, with At-anan Yomchinda's design incorporating varied terrain and enough water to demand accurate approach play. The signature moments of a round at Evergreen Hills come on holes 16 and 17, both of which feature island greens — a genuine test that requires a committed shot over open water to hold the putting surface. These two holes alone generate the kind of nervous-excitement dynamic that makes a course memorable well after the round. Night golf is available at Evergreen Hills, which extends playing options for resort guests and local members during the hotter months when an evening tee time is preferable to a midday round. A driving range is on-site for pre-round practice.
prose.tips: The two island-green holes (16 and 17) are the moments most golfers remember from a round at Evergreen Hills — take an extra club and commit to the shot rather than steering it. At 6,879 yards, the course has enough length to challenge mid-handicappers from the back tees, so consider moving up a set of tees if you are not a big hitter. The resort's 77-room accommodation means staying overnight is convenient, and a two-day golf package pairing Evergreen Hills with another Kanchanaburi course is a popular itinerary for visiting groups. Night golf availability is worth confirming with the club directly, as schedules can vary by season. Caddies at 300 THB represent good value given the course's water features.
prose.location_and_access: Evergreen Hills Golf Club & Resort is located in Tha Muang District, Kanchanaburi province, approximately 200 km from Bangkok. Despite the greater distance compared to some western-region courses, the route is relatively fast: the club is reachable in approximately two hours from Bangkok via the main highway toward Kanchanaburi. From Kanchanaburi town itself, the drive to the course is around 50 minutes. Tha Muang District sits south of Kanchanaburi town along Route 323, placing the resort in a rural corridor that receives significantly less traffic than the capital's outer ring roads. The combination of resort accommodation and night golf makes Evergreen Hills a practical destination for multi-day golf trips originating from Bangkok.
prose.rental_cta_context: LENGOLF offers rental sets at 900 THB that can supplement or replace travel equipment for a round at Evergreen Hills Golf Club & Resort in Kanchanaburi. Arranging a rental through LENGOLF before departing Bangkok is a straightforward option for visiting golfers who prefer to travel light.

en.meta_description: Evergreen Hills Golf Club: 6,879-yard par-72 in Kanchanaburi with island greens on holes 16 & 17, from 800 THB. 2 hrs from Bangkok. Club hire 900 THB.
